channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Canberra riders turn to bikes as fuel costs bite
item.description: Rising petrol and diesel prices are prompting more Canberrans to leave their cars at home and get back on their bikes. Local bike shop co-owner Tim Palmer says sales and repairs at Rideshop in Braddon...

item.title: Rising fuel prices linked to Iran conflict drive increase in petrol thefts in the UK
item.description: Petrol theft in the United Kingdom has increased significantly, rising by around 62% compared with the previous year, according to data compiled for the BBC by fuel theft recovery company Pay My Fuel....

item.title: Presidency confirms Ramaphosa will prioritise Malema’s Fort Hare investigation letter amid denial controversy
item.description: The Presidency has confirmed that President Cyril Ramaphosa will prioritise a letter from EFF leader Julius Malema concerning alleged academic fraud at the University of Fort Hare, following an initia...

item.title: Practical alternatives to airtime and data loans after MTN and Airtel suspension in Nigeria
item.description: MTN Nigeria and Airtel have recently suspended their airtime and data loan services, popularly known as XtraTime, leaving many subscribers without the usual quick option to borrow credit when they run...

item.title: Amazon’s Eero Outdoor 7 extends backyard connectivity but raises practical concerns
item.description: The article reviews Amazon’s Eero Outdoor 7, a Wi-Fi extender designed to push reliable internet coverage well beyond the walls of a home and into outdoor spaces like backyards. The device promises to...

item.title: Proposed eviction law aims to support small landlords and strengthen municipal role in illegal occupation cases
item.description: The South African government is proposing changes to eviction laws that could significantly assist small-scale landlords, particularly those renting out backyard rooms in townships and informal settle...

item.title: Usain Bolt advises rising sprinter Gout Gout on handling pressure and transitioning to senior competition
item.description: Usain Bolt has offered guidance to rising Australian sprint star Gout Gout as the teenager prepares for a major step up into senior international competition. Gout, just 18, recently drew global atten...

item.title: Tech entrepreneur alleges EFCC harassment and prolonged seizure of assets without charge
item.description: A Nigerian tech entrepreneur, Akukoma Israel, has publicly accused the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) of harassment and unlawful detention of his personal and business belongings for ...

item.title: Police arrest three suspects linked to robbery and sexual assault incident in Butere
item.description: Police in Kenya have arrested three suspects believed to be involved in a serious case of robbery with violence and gang rape that occurred in Butere. According to the National Police Service, detecti...

item.title: Kabras Family Shares Cultural Reasons Behind Restricted Attendance in Accident Victim’s Burial
item.description: A family from Kakamega has opened up about a burial practice that left many Kenyans talking, after they buried a relative who died in a road accident under strict traditional customs. According to the...

item.title: Rare wildlife sighting of big cats reportedly observed together in Ranthambore Tiger Reserve
item.description: The article appears to describe a rare wildlife sighting reported from the Ranthambore Tiger Reserve, where multiple large wild cats were allegedly seen in the same region. According to the available ...

item.title: Ford's Private Jet Plan Aborted After Public Backlash
item.description: Premier Doug Ford of Ontario has abruptly scrapped plans to purchase a $28.9 million Bombardier Challenger jet for official use, following a swift and intense public backlash. The proposal, initially ...

item.title: US-Iran tensions rise as negotiations stall and both sides signal military readiness
item.description: Nagbabala ang United States at Iran na handa silang pumasok sa digmaan habang nananatiling hindi umuusad ang diplomatic talks sa pagitan ng dalawang bansa. Ayon sa ulat, parehong panig ay nagpapakita ...

item.title: Bodyguard linked to Sydney crime network abducted and recovered within hours amid gang conflict
item.description: A man identified as Emilio Chalhoub, reportedly working as a bodyguard for Sydney rapper Ay Huncho, was allegedly abducted from his family home in Guildford late on Monday night in what police believe...

item.title: Worcester court hands life sentence for rape and multiple violent assaults
item.description: The Worcester Regional Court has sentenced 31-year-old Gerald Joseph to an effective life term following his conviction on serious charges including rape, kidnapping, and multiple counts of assault wi...

item.title: BJP’s cultural outreach and ‘outsider’ image challenge in West Bengal politics
item.description: The article examines how the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) is attempting to overcome its long-standing perception as an ‘outsider’ party in West Bengal ahead of the 2026 elections. A key focus of the c...

item.title: Pope Leo condemns exploitation and corruption during Angola pastoral visit
item.description: Pope Leo XIV has criticised exploitation, corruption, and injustice by powerful individuals during his visit to Angola’s diamond-rich northeastern region. The American pope, on the eighth day of his 1...

item.title: Nigeria Bans Import of 17 Essential Goods from Non-ECOWAS Countries Effective April 2026
item.description: The federal government of Nigeria has introduced a revised import prohibition list that bans the importation of 17 essential goods from countries outside the Economic Community of West African States ...

item.title: Iran hesitates on US peace talks amid ongoing blockade; Apple announces leadership change with John Ternus as CEO successor
item.description: The Morning Digest brings together key international developments spanning geopolitics and global business. In a significant geopolitical update, Iran has stated that it has not yet taken a decision o...

item.title: Tim Cook to Step Down as Apple CEO After 15 Years, John Ternus Named Successor
item.description: Apple has announced that CEO Tim Cook will step down from his role in September 2026 after leading the company for 15 years. He will be succeeded by John Ternus, Apple’s senior vice president of hardw...
